title: Scalable dual-prefix hybrid scorer rerun

queries: candidate aware multiclass dual view semantic raw label class id shared prefix scorer scalable rerun
autocomplete multiclass dual prefix shared prefix hybrid rerun implementation fix

evidence.why_now: EXP111 was implementation-blocked rather than scientifically failed: user-churn and user-ltv completed, while item-incoterms crashed before its first evaluation because the dual-prefix path instantiated the fusion gate but not the pairwise scorer. The code path is now fixed, so the next correct action is a clean rerun of the same candidate family.

hypothesis: With the dual-prefix initialization bug fixed, a clean rerun of the scalable dual-prefix hybrid scorer should test the intended architecture: pairwise candidate-aware interaction plus shared-prefix scoring over both class-id and raw-label token views, without returning to exhaustive O(L*C) rollout.
notes: This is a clean rerun of EXP111 after fixing the missing pairwise-scorer initialization bug on the dual-prefix path.
Do not mix any new loss, sampling, packaging, or pretrain changes into this rerun.
Launched on 2026-05-08 through the local Stage 3 launcher.
Resolved idle-GPU placement at launch time: user-churn -> lab25211 GPU 0, user-ltv -> lab25211 GPU 5, item-incoterms -> lab25211 GPU 6.
Follow-up orchestrator status check after launch showed all three windows up and logs in DB-load startup.
Completed on 2026-05-08 as a resource-blocked bundle.
user-churn and user-ltv finished, but item-incoterms ran out of memory before the first evaluation on the raw-label shared-prefix path.
Treat this as a memory-scaling failure in the current implementation rather than scientific evidence against the dual-prefix family.
